title: RoadDarts.com
description: Custom MERN Stack web application for business location discovery and navigation with Google Maps integration.
longDescription: Built a complete custom web application using MERN stack (MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js) that helps users discover and navigate to business locations effortlessly. Integrated Google Maps API for real-time directions, advanced filtering functionality, and visual display of business locations on interactive maps.
challenge: The client needed a platform that could handle thousands of business listings, allow users to filter by category and proximity, and show real-time routing — all within a single-page app that felt fast and native on mobile.
solution: Built a full MERN stack application with a custom REST API, MongoDB geospatial indexing for proximity searches, React frontend with state management, and Google Maps JS API integration for dynamic routing and cluster markers.

title: GlobalRemote.nl
description: Automated recruitment platform with n8n workflows and intelligent CV parsing for the Dutch market.
longDescription: Built key components of GlobalRemote's automated recruitment platform using n8n workflows. Designed custom registration pages and integrated powerful CV parsing module that automatically extracts and processes candidate resumes. Created automated backend workflows that trigger when applications are submitted.
challenge: The recruitment team was spending 8+ hours per day manually processing CVs and entering candidate data. They needed an automated pipeline that could extract key information from uploaded resumes and route candidates to the right job categories.
solution: Implemented a WordPress front-end with custom registration forms, then connected n8n workflows that trigger on form submission. The n8n pipeline parses CVs, extracts candidate details, and pushes structured data into their CRM while sending automated follow-up emails.
results: CV parsing pipeline live in 3 weeks

title: Talehouse
description: Fully custom WordPress theme built from scratch with flexible content modules and a polished editorial design.
longDescription: Developed a custom WordPress theme built from scratch based on client requirements. Features flexible dynamic sections, custom layouts, and reusable content modules that enable easy management and updates. The solution supports multiple iterations and evolving content workflows.
challenge: The client had outgrown off-the-shelf themes but didn't want a page builder. They needed a completely custom theme that gave editors full control over content layout without touching code.
solution: Built a bespoke WordPress theme using PHP, advanced custom fields (ACF), and custom post types. Created a library of flexible content modules — each with its own layout options — that the editorial team could compose into any page structure.

title: Novara MD
description: Pixel-perfect WordPress conversion from a detailed Figma design for a medical aesthetics brand.
longDescription: Meticulously translated a detailed Figma design into a fully functional WordPress site. Every layout detail, typography specification, and component was implemented with pixel-perfect accuracy across all breakpoints.
challenge: The design team had invested weeks in a highly detailed Figma prototype — every pixel, every animation, every hover state was specified. The client wanted code that honored 100% of that design without compromise.
solution: Used WordPress with Elementor Pro and heavy custom CSS to match every design detail. Implemented custom hover animations, responsive typography scales, and component-level CSS overrides to achieve exact fidelity to the Figma source.

title: CleverConcept.nl
description: Case studies showcase with dynamic filtering, custom post types, and interactive browsing experience.
longDescription: Focused on building the main Case Studies page with pixel-perfect design accuracy. Developed a dynamic filtering system allowing easy browsing of case categories. The result is an engaging, interactive showcase that improves usability and makes it easier for visitors to explore work samples.
challenge: The existing case studies page was a static list that was hard to browse and impossible to filter. The client needed a system where adding new case studies was simple, and visitors could filter by industry or service type.
solution: Built a custom post type for case studies with taxonomy-based categories, custom meta fields for project details, and a JavaScript filter system that shows/hides cards without page reload. All styled to match the existing brand precisely.

title: WeTotalCare
description: Premium WooCommerce store built from Figma with custom product sliders and a polished shopping experience.
longDescription: Built a custom WordPress + WooCommerce store from a detailed Figma design. Special attention was paid to premium UI features including a custom product slider on the homepage using advanced addon plugins. Every page was built to match the design with pixel-perfect accuracy.
challenge: The client had a high-end product line but their existing WooCommerce site felt generic. They needed a complete rebuild that reflected the premium nature of their brand, with custom product presentation and a better purchase flow.
solution: Rebuilt from scratch using WordPress + WooCommerce with a custom child theme. Implemented a premium product slider with custom transitions, bespoke product page templates with enlarged media galleries, and a streamlined cart and checkout flow.

title: GC Logistics
description: High-end logistics company website with a luxury aesthetic, custom animations, and a powerful service showcase.
longDescription: Designed and built a premium website for a logistics company targeting enterprise clients. The brief was 'luxury logistics' — the site needed to convey precision, reliability, and scale while looking visually impressive. Built with WordPress using a custom theme and advanced CSS animations.
challenge: Logistics websites are typically dry and corporate. The client wanted to stand out in a crowded market by looking premium and trustworthy, with a design that matched their high-value service proposition.
solution: Custom WordPress theme built around a dark, sophisticated color palette with gold accents. Implemented smooth scroll animations, a dynamic service card grid, interactive fleet showcase, and an animated metrics section showing company stats.

title: House of Greens
description: Organic plant nursery e-commerce site with WooCommerce, a natural earthy aesthetic, and a seamless shop experience.
longDescription: Built a full WooCommerce store for an organic plant and gardening brand. The design required a warm, natural feel with earthy tones, product photography showcasing, and an intuitive browsing experience. Implemented custom plant category navigation and a streamlined checkout.
challenge: The client had beautiful photography and quality products but an outdated website that didn't showcase either. They needed a modern e-commerce experience that felt aligned with their organic, natural brand values.
solution: WordPress + WooCommerce with a custom theme using earthy typography and color palette. Built custom product category pages with large photography, a sticky quick-add-to-cart feature, and a simplified 2-step checkout with plant care information on product pages.

title: Karma Fast Food
description: Bold restaurant brand website with online menu, location finder, and an energetic visual identity.
longDescription: Built a brand-forward website for a fast food restaurant chain. The site needed high energy, bold typography, and a mouth-watering visual experience. Implemented an interactive menu with category filters, a location finder, and an online ordering integration.
challenge: The client had strong brand photography and a bold identity but no web presence that matched it. They needed a site that felt as exciting as their brand — fast, visual, appetizing — and drove foot traffic and online orders.
solution: Custom WordPress site with a bold design system — high-contrast colors, large food photography, and animated menu cards. Integrated an interactive menu with JavaScript filtering by category, a location finder with embedded maps, and an online ordering plugin.

title: Lobos & Co
description: Creative agency portfolio site with editorial layout, smooth animations, and a strong visual identity.
longDescription: Designed and developed a sophisticated agency website for Lobos & Co. The client wanted something that felt premium, editorial, and confident — the kind of site that wins clients before a word is read. Built with WordPress and custom animations.
challenge: Agency websites need to sell the agency before showcasing the work. Lobos needed a site that immediately communicated quality, professionalism, and creativity — not just a portfolio list.
solution: Built a custom WordPress site with an editorial design system: large typography, generous whitespace, scroll-triggered reveal animations, and a curated work showcase. The homepage leads with a bold statement, followed by selected case studies with hover effects.

title: Manyway Laundry
description: Modern laundry service website with online booking, service packages, and a clean approachable design.
longDescription: Built a complete web presence for a laundry and dry-cleaning service. The site needed to feel modern and trustworthy while making it extremely easy for customers to book pickups and understand pricing. Implemented an online booking flow integrated with the client's scheduling system.
challenge: The client was losing business to competitors with better digital presence. Customers would visit the site, not understand the service packages quickly, and leave without booking.
solution: Redesigned the site with a clear pricing table, a 3-step booking process, and social proof sections. Connected an online booking widget with automatic SMS confirmation. Simplified the service descriptions and added before/after photography.

title: Panacea Care
description: Professional healthcare website with service pages, appointment booking, and trust-building patient experience.
longDescription: Built a comprehensive healthcare website for Panacea Care, covering multiple service areas including physiotherapy, nutrition, and wellness coaching. The site needed to feel trustworthy, clinical, and warm — a difficult balance that required careful design choices.
challenge: Healthcare sites need to balance professionalism with approachability. Patients should feel reassured and confident, not intimidated. The client also needed online appointment booking and clear service descriptions for SEO.
solution: Custom WordPress site with a clean, light design using calming colors. Built individual service pages with structured content, implemented an appointment booking plugin with calendar sync, and added patient testimonials and team profiles to build trust.

title: ShopFitters Fitout
description: Premium retail fitout company website showcasing completed projects, services, and driving B2B enquiries.
longDescription: Built a polished B2B website for a retail shop fitting company. Their clients are retail brands, restaurant chains, and commercial spaces looking for fitout expertise. The site needed to showcase craftsmanship, convey scale, and generate quality leads.
challenge: ShopFitters were winning business through referrals only. They needed a digital presence that could showcase their portfolio quality, communicate their process, and generate direct enquiries from brand managers and procurement teams.
solution: Built a project portfolio site with a custom filterable case study grid, a detailed 'Our Process' page showing the end-to-end fitout journey, and a conversion-optimized enquiry form. Heavy use of full-bleed photography to showcase the quality of completed fit-outs.

title: Furniture Store
description: Premium furniture e-commerce concept with 3D product mockups, clean editorial layout, and a luxe shopping experience.
longDescription: Developed a high-end furniture e-commerce concept showcasing premium product photography and a minimalist editorial aesthetic. The concept demonstrates clean typography hierarchy, generous whitespace, 3D product presentations, and an intuitive category browsing system.
challenge: Premium furniture brands need their website to match the quality of their physical showrooms. Generic WooCommerce themes undercut the brand. This concept solves that by treating the website as an extension of the showroom experience.
solution: Built a concept using Next.js and Tailwind CSS with a bespoke design system. Large product photography, sophisticated typography, micro-interaction hover states, and a room-scene product visualizer demonstrate how luxury furniture should be presented online.

title: Yogi Escape
description: Serene yoga studio website with class booking, instructor profiles, and a calming visual meditation aesthetic.
longDescription: Built a wellness-focused website for a yoga studio and retreat brand. The site needed to evoke calm, flow, and mindfulness while still being functional — booking classes, showcasing instructors, and selling retreat packages. Every design decision was made to reduce friction and increase serenity.
challenge: Most yoga studio websites feel either too corporate or too DIY. Yogi Escape needed something that felt premium, peaceful, and made the visitor want to book a class within 30 seconds of arriving on the page.
solution: Custom WordPress site with soft, organic design language — curved elements, warm neutrals, flowing typography. Implemented a class schedule with online booking, instructor profile pages with specialty tags, and a retreat packages page with integrated payment.

title: Drivco
description: Custom PHP car dealership and live auction platform with a real-time bidding engine and admin management panel.
longDescription: Built a full car dealership and auction platform from scratch in PHP and MySQL — no framework, no page builder. Covers the full flow a real auction site needs: browsing by brand and category, viewing detailed listings with bid history, placing live bids against a countdown, and an admin panel for managing inventory.
challenge: A car marketplace is a different kind of project than a brochure site — it needed a real relational data model (cars, bids, users, orders, cart, wishlist) and bidding logic that has to behave correctly under time pressure: tracking the current highest bid and bidder, counting down to auction close, and preventing stale data from showing the wrong price.
solution: Designed an 8-table MySQL schema (cardetails, bids, users, orders, cart, wishlist, products, admins) and built the full PHP application around it — brand/category browsing, a detailed auction page with a live countdown timer and current-highest-bid display, an admin login and management panel, and a Tailwind-based UI on top of it all.
results: Real-time bidding engine with live highest-bid tracking
8-table relational schema covering the full auction lifecycle
Admin panel for listing & user management
keyFeatures: Live bidding engine with countdown timers

title: Kings Capital
description: React + Tailwind website for a wealth management and financial advisory firm.
longDescription: Built a corporate website for a financial advisory firm covering financial planning, investment management, retirement solutions, and wealth preservation. The brief called for a site that reads as trustworthy and established — the kind of design that reassures a visitor handing over financial decisions to a stranger.
challenge: Financial services sites have to walk a line: look credible and established without tipping into generic stock-photo corporate cliché, and present four distinct service lines clearly enough that a visitor can self-select into the right one without a phone call.
solution: Built a React frontend styled entirely with a custom Tailwind config, structured around a clear service-first information architecture — a hero with a direct value statement, a four-pillar services grid (Financial Planning, Investment Management, Retirement Solutions, Wealth Preservation) repeated as a comparison section, and a testimonials block for social proof.
results: Four service lines presented with clear self-selection paths
Consistent design system built on custom Tailwind tokens
Client testimonials integrated for trust signals

title: KPEG
description: React + Framer Motion site for a private equity holding company, built with page transitions and motion-driven storytelling.
longDescription: Built a corporate site for a private equity group that owns several lines of business under one umbrella. The brief was explicitly about feel as much as function — a confident, cinematic site using motion to communicate scale and global reach, not just a static page of investment categories.
challenge: Private equity sites tend to be either dry and text-heavy or flashy without substance. The goal here was a site that felt genuinely premium through motion design — page transitions, scroll-driven reveals — while still clearly communicating the firm's investment focus areas (Global Presence, Strategic Partnerships, Financial Expertise).
solution: Built entirely in React with Framer Motion handling page transitions and scroll-triggered animations, layered over full-bleed photography of skylines and business environments to reinforce the 'global reach' positioning. Structured navigation around the firm's core pillars rather than generic page names.
results: Custom page-transition system built with Framer Motion
Motion-driven storytelling replacing static content blocks
Clear navigation around investment focus pillars

title: Veterans Transition Support
description: Technical SEO audit, key page redesigns, and a custom job-landings feature for a nonprofit veteran job-placement organization.
longDescription: Ongoing engagement with Veterans Transition Support, a nonprofit helping veterans move into civilian careers through free job training and mentoring. The work spans a full technical SEO audit, redesigns of several key pages (donors & sponsors, events calendar, donation flow, job submission form), and a custom 'job landings' feature that lets the team log and showcase veteran job placements with an approval workflow.
challenge: As a donor- and grant-funded nonprofit, the site needed to be both technically healthy for search visibility and credible to prospective donors and partners — while giving a non-technical team a way to log job placements (the organization's core success metric) without needing a developer involved every time.
solution: Ran a full crawl-based technical SEO audit (accessibility, Core Web Vitals, structured data, canonicals, redirects, and 200+ other checks) and used it to drive a prioritized cleanup plan. Delivered redesigns for the donors/sponsors, events, donation, and job submission pages with formal client sign-off on each. Built a custom job-landings board with a submission and admin-approval workflow so the team can publish new veteran placements themselves. Separately scoped a phased growth proposal covering a homepage rebuild, an Events Hub via the Eventbrite API, and automated lead capture.
results: Full technical SEO audit covering 200+ automated checks
Four key pages redesigned with formal client approval
Custom job-landings board with self-service publishing
Phased growth roadmap scoped for homepage, events, and automation
keyFeatures: Key page redesigns with client approval workflow
